methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Evolv Technology (NASDAQ: EVLV) is a public-stage Defense & Security company that has raised over $400M. The relevant comp universe consists of 21 same-sector peers, including 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms spanning the frontier-tech security space, with notable private comparables including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

The peer-median triangulation method is not applicable to this valuation. Despite 21 peers operating in Defense & Security, filtering by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier yields fewer than the required 3 datapoints needed for statistical reliability. No same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier peers met the threshold. An all-sector median approach would introduce substantial noise given the heterogeneity of Defense & Security subsegments and company profiles.

This analysis exposes a core constraint: Evolv occupies a narrow subsegment within Defense & Security where direct comparables are either too distant in stage, capital efficiency, or business model to anchor meaningful valuation ranges. Alternative methods—including discounted cash flow analysis, revenue multiples applied to specific peer subsets, or transaction comps—would be required to triangulate a defensible range. The current public status provides direct market pricing, but relative valuation techniques cannot be reliably applied given the sparse comparable set.
